speaker: PUCELLE
text: These are the city gates, the gates of Roan,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Through which our policy must make a breach.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Take heed. Be wary how you place your words;

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Talk like the vulgar sort of market men

speaker: PUCELLE
text: That come to gather money for their corn.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: If we have entrance, as I hope we shall,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And that we find the slothful watch but weak,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: I’ll by a sign give notice to our friends,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: That Charles the Dauphin may encounter them.

speaker: SOLDIER
text: Our sacks shall be a mean to sack the city,

speaker: SOLDIER
text: And we be lords and rulers over Roan;

speaker: SOLDIER
text: Therefore we’ll knock.

speaker: WATCH
text: Qui là?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Paysans la pauvre gens de France:

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Poor market folks that come to sell their corn.

speaker: WATCH
text: Enter, go in. The market bell is rung.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Now, Roan, I’ll shake thy bulwarks to the ground.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Saint Dennis bless this happy stratagem

speaker: CHARLES
text: And once again we’ll sleep secure in Roan.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Here entered Pucelle and her practisants.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Now she is there, how will she specify

speaker: BASTARD
text: “Here is the best and safest passage in”?

speaker: REIGNIER
text: By thrusting out a torch from yonder tower,

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Which, once discerned, shows that her meaning is:

speaker: REIGNIER
text: No way to that, for weakness, which she entered.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Behold, this is the happy wedding torch

speaker: PUCELLE
text: That joineth Roan unto her countrymen,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: But burning fatal to the Talbonites.

speaker: BASTARD
text: See, noble Charles, the beacon of our friend;

speaker: BASTARD
text: The burning torch, in yonder turret stands.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Now shine it like a comet of revenge,

speaker: CHARLES
text: A prophet to the fall of all our foes!

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Defer no time; delays have dangerous ends.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Enter and cry “The Dauphin!” presently,

speaker: REIGNIER
text: And then do execution on the watch.

speaker: TALBOT
text: France, thou shalt rue this treason with thy tears,

speaker: TALBOT
text: If Talbot but survive thy treachery.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Pucelle, that witch, that damnèd sorceress,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Hath wrought this hellish mischief unawares,

speaker: TALBOT
text: That hardly we escaped the pride of France.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Good morrow, gallants. Want you corn for bread?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: I think the Duke of Burgundy will fast

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Before he’ll buy again at such a rate.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: ’Twas full of darnel. Do you like the taste?

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Scoff on, vile fiend and shameless courtesan!

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: I trust ere long to choke thee with thine own,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: And make thee curse the harvest of that corn.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Your Grace may starve, perhaps, before that time.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: O, let no words, but deeds, revenge this treason.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: What will you do, good graybeard? Break a lance

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And run a-tilt at Death within a chair?

speaker: TALBOT
text: Foul fiend of France and hag of all despite,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Encompassed with thy lustful paramours,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Becomes it thee to taunt his valiant age

speaker: TALBOT
text: And twit with cowardice a man half dead?

speaker: TALBOT
text: Damsel, I’ll have a bout with you again,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Or else let Talbot perish with this shame.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Are you so hot, sir? Yet, Pucelle, hold thy peace,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: If Talbot do but thunder, rain will follow.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: God speed the Parliament! Who shall be the Speaker?

speaker: TALBOT
text: Dare you come forth and meet us in the field?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Belike your Lordship takes us then for fools,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: To try if that our own be ours or no.

speaker: TALBOT
text: I speak not to that railing Hecate,

speaker: TALBOT
text: But unto thee, Alanson, and the rest.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Will you, like soldiers, come and fight it out?

speaker: ALANSON
text: Seigneur, no.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Seigneur, hang! Base muleteers of France,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Like peasant footboys do they keep the walls

speaker: TALBOT
text: And dare not take up arms like gentlemen.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Away, captains. Let’s get us from the walls,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: For Talbot means no goodness by his looks.—

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Goodbye, my lord. We came but to tell you

speaker: PUCELLE
text: That we are here.

speaker: TALBOT
text: And there will we be too, ere it be long,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Or else reproach be Talbot’s greatest fame.—

speaker: TALBOT
text: Vow, Burgundy, by honor of thy house,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Pricked on by public wrongs sustained in France,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Either to get the town again or die.

speaker: TALBOT
text: And I, as sure as English Henry lives,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And as his father here was conqueror,

speaker: TALBOT
text: As sure as in this late-betrayèd town

speaker: TALBOT
text: Great Coeur-de-lion’s heart was burièd,

speaker: TALBOT
text: So sure I swear to get the town or die.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: My vows are equal partners with thy vows.

speaker: TALBOT
text: But, ere we go, regard this dying prince,

speaker: TALBOT
text: The valiant Duke of Bedford.—Come, my lord,

speaker: TALBOT
text: We will bestow you in some better place,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Fitter for sickness and for crazy age.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Lord Talbot, do not so dishonor me.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Here will I sit, before the walls of Roan,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: And will be partner of your weal or woe.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Courageous Bedford, let us now persuade you—

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Not to be gone from hence, for once I read

speaker: BEDFORD
text: That stout Pendragon, in his litter sick,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Came to the field and vanquishèd his foes.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Methinks I should revive the soldiers’ hearts

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Because I ever found them as myself.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Undaunted spirit in a dying breast,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Then be it so. Heavens keep old Bedford safe!—

speaker: TALBOT
text: And now no more ado, brave Burgundy,

speaker: TALBOT
text: But gather we our forces out of hand

speaker: TALBOT
text: And set upon our boasting enemy.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Whither away, Sir John Fastolf, in such haste?

speaker: FASTOLF
text: Whither away? To save myself by flight.

speaker: FASTOLF
text: We are like to have the overthrow again.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: What, will you fly and leave Lord Talbot?

speaker: FASTOLF
text: Ay,

speaker: FASTOLF
text: All the Talbots in the world, to save my life.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Cowardly knight, ill fortune follow thee.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Now, quiet soul, depart when heaven please,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: For I have seen our enemies’ overthrow.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: What is the trust or strength of foolish man?

speaker: BEDFORD
text: They that of late were daring with their scoffs

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Are glad and fain by flight to save themselves.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Lost and recovered in a day again!

speaker: TALBOT
text: This is a double honor, Burgundy.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Yet heavens have glory for this victory.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Warlike and martial Talbot, Burgundy

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Enshrines thee in his heart, and there erects

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Thy noble deeds as valor’s monuments.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Thanks, gentle duke. But where is Pucelle now?

speaker: TALBOT
text: I think her old familiar is asleep.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Now where’s the Bastard’s braves and Charles his gleeks?

speaker: TALBOT
text: What, all amort? Roan hangs her head for grief

speaker: TALBOT
text: That such a valiant company are fled.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Now will we take some order in the town,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Placing therein some expert officers,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And then depart to Paris to the King,

speaker: TALBOT
text: For there young Henry with his nobles lie.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: What wills Lord Talbot pleaseth Burgundy.

speaker: TALBOT
text: But yet, before we go, let’s not forget

speaker: TALBOT
text: The noble Duke of Bedford late-deceased,

speaker: TALBOT
text: But see his exequies fulfilled in Roan.

speaker: TALBOT
text: A braver soldier never couchèd lance,

speaker: TALBOT
text: A gentler heart did never sway in court.

speaker: TALBOT
text: But kings and mightiest potentates must die,

speaker: TALBOT
text: For that’s the end of human misery.
